description: Solidifies core 6th grade standards while introducing 7th grade concepts at preview level.

skills.name: Rational Numbers on the Number Line

templates.problem: Plot {a} and {b} on a number line. Which is greater?
Order these numbers from least to greatest: {a}, {b}, {c}, {d}
What is the absolute value of {a}?
The temperature on Monday was {a}°F. On Tuesday it was {b}°F. Which day was colder? How much colder?
A submarine is at {a} feet and a fish is at {b} feet. Which is deeper? What is the difference in their depths?
Which of these numbers is between {a} and {b} on a number line: {c}, {d}, {e}?
Write a negative number that represents: a bank account overdrawn by ${a}.
A number line shows points at {a}, {b}, and {c}. Describe in words where each point is relative to zero.
A number and its absolute value are both {a}. What could the number be? Give both answers.
A student said '{a} > {b} because {a} is farther from zero.' Are they correct? Explain.
Use <, >, or = to compare: |{a}| ___ |{b}|
What is the distance between {a} and {b} on a number line?
Name three negative numbers greater than {a}.
A business had profits and losses over 4 days: {a}, {b}, {c}, {d} dollars. On which days did they lose money?
Write the opposite of {a}. Then write the opposite of the opposite.

templates.constraints_note: b != 0, a must be divisible by b

templates.problem: Without calculating, will {a} × {b} be positive or negative? Explain your reasoning.
The temperature dropped {a}°F each hour for {b} hours. What was the total change in temperature?
You owe ${a} on {b} library books. Write a multiplication equation and find how much you owe in total.
A student says {a} - {b} = {wrong}. What is the correct answer and what did they likely do wrong?
templates.generation_note: wrong = a + b (student added instead of subtracting a negative)

templates.problem: __ × {a} = {product}. Find the missing number.
Without calculating, which is greater: {a} × {b} or {a} × {c}? Explain.
A football team gained {a} yards, lost {b} yards, gained {c} yards, and lost {d} yards. What was the net yardage?
Write two integers whose product is {a} and whose sum is negative.
The table shows a stock's daily change: Day 1: {a}, Day 2: {b}, Day 3: {c}. Write an expression and find the total change.

skills.name: Ratios, Unit Rates, and Proportional Relationships

templates.problem: Write the ratio of {a} to {b} in three ways: using a colon, as a fraction, and in words.
Simplify the ratio {a}:{b} to lowest terms.
Are the ratios {a}:{b} and {c}:{d} equivalent? Show your work.
A car travels {a} miles in {b} hours. Find the unit rate in miles per hour.
templates.constraints_note: a must be divisible by b

templates.problem: Store A sells {a} items for ${b}. Store B sells {c} items for ${d}. Which is the better deal per item?
A recipe for {a} people uses {b} cups of flour. How much flour is needed for {c} people?
A map scale is 1 inch = {a} miles. Two cities are {b} inches apart on the map. How far apart are they in miles?
Complete the ratio table: if {a} tickets cost ${b}, fill in the cost for {c}, {d}, and {e} tickets.
A unit rate is {a} miles per gallon. How many gallons are needed to travel {b} miles?
A student says the unit rate for {a} items for ${b} is ${c} per item. Is that correct? What is the right unit rate?
templates.generation_note: c = b * a (student multiplied instead of divided)

templates.problem: A player scored {a} points in {b} games. At this rate, how many points would they score in {c} games?
Write your own ratio problem where the unit rate is {a} miles per hour. Include a question and answer.
In a class, {a} out of {b} students prefer math. Write this as a ratio, a fraction, and a percent.
Orange juice is mixed in a ratio of {a} parts juice to {b} parts water. If you use {c} cups of water, how much juice do you need?
Runner A finishes {a} km in {b} minutes. Runner B finishes {c} km in {d} minutes. Who is faster?
Evaluate {a}x + {b} when x = {c}.
Solve for x: x + {a} = {b}
Solve for x: {a}x = {b}
Solve for x: {a}x + {b} = {c}
Write an algebraic expression for: {a} more than twice a number.
Write an equation for: {a} times a number decreased by {b} equals {c}. Then solve it.
You earn ${a} per hour. After working some hours you have ${b}. Write and solve an equation for the number of hours worked.
A rectangle has perimeter {a} cm. Its width is {b} cm. Write and solve an equation for its length.
Which value of x makes {a}x - {b} = {c} true: x = {w}, x = {r}, or x = {s}?
templates.generation_note: One of w, r, s satisfies the equation exactly

templates.problem: A student solved {a}x + {b} = {c} and got x = {wrong}. What did they do wrong? What is the correct answer?
templates.generation_note: wrong = c/a (student ignored adding b first)

templates.problem: When x = {a}, the expression {b}x + c equals {result}. Find c.
Evaluate {a}x² when x = {b}. (Note: x² means x times x.)
Maya is {a} years older than her brother. If her brother is b years old, write an expression for Maya's age. If b = {c}, how old is Maya?
Write a word problem that could be solved with the equation {a}x + {b} = {c}. Then solve it.
Expression A: {a}x + {b}. Expression B: {c}x - {d}. For x = {e}, which expression has the greater value?
Find the area of a triangle with base {a} cm and height {b} cm.
Find the area of a parallelogram with base {a} units and height {b} units.
Find the surface area of a rectangular prism with length {a}, width {b}, and height {c}.
Find the volume of a rectangular prism with length {a}, width {b}, and height {c}.
A gift box is {a} inches long, {b} inches wide, and {c} inches tall. How many square inches of wrapping paper are needed to cover it completely?
A triangular garden has a base of {a} feet and a height of {b} feet. How many square feet of sod are needed to cover it?
A rectangle has area {a} square units. Its length is {b} units. Find its width.
A rectangular prism has volume {a} cubic inches and a base area of {b} square inches. What is its height?
Shape A is a triangle with base {a} and height {b}. Shape B is a rectangle with length {c} and width {d}. Which has the greater area?
A student found the area of a triangle with base {a} and height {b} to be {wrong} square units. What did they do wrong? What is the correct area?
templates.generation_note: wrong = a * b (forgot to multiply by 1/2)

templates.problem: An L-shaped room consists of two rectangles: one {a} by {b} feet and one {c} by {d} feet. Find the total floor area.
An aquarium is {a} feet long, {b} feet wide, and {c} feet tall. What is the volume of water it can hold?
Design a rectangular prism with a volume of {a} cubic units. Give its length, width, and height.
A net is unfolded from a box. It shows {a} rectangles. Two are {b} × {c} and four are {b} × {d} or {c} × {d}. What is the surface area of the box?
Box A has dimensions {a} × {b} × {c}. Box B has dimensions {d} × {e} × {f}. Which box holds more? By how much?
Find the distance between ({a}, {y}) and ({b}, {y}) using absolute value.
Find the distance between ({x}, {a}) and ({x}, {b}) using absolute value.
In which quadrant is the point ({a}, {b})? What are the signs of the coordinates in that quadrant?
On a city map, the library is at ({a}, {b}) and the park is at ({c}, {b}). Each unit = {s} blocks. How many blocks apart are they?
A game piece starts at ({a}, {b}) and moves to ({a}, {c}). How many units did it move? In which direction?
Point A is at ({a}, {b}). Point B is {d} units directly to the right of A. What are the coordinates of B?
Point M is at ({mx}, {my}). Is it closer to point A ({ax}, {ay}) or point B ({bx}, {by})? Use absolute value to justify.
A rectangle has vertices at ({a}, {b}), ({c}, {b}), ({c}, {d}), and ({a}, {d}). Find its perimeter.
A rectangle has vertices at ({a}, {b}), ({c}, {b}), ({c}, {d}), and ({a}, {d}). Find its area.
A student says the distance between ({a}, {y}) and ({b}, {y}) is {wrong}. What is correct? What did they do wrong?
templates.generation_note: wrong = a + b (student added instead of using absolute value of difference)

templates.problem: Plot and label these points: A({a},{b}), B({c},{d}), C({e},{f}). Which two are in the same quadrant?
Name two points in Quadrant III that are exactly {d} units apart on a horizontal line.
A ship sails from ({a}, {b}) due north {c} units, then due east {d} units. What are its final coordinates?
What is the perimeter of a triangle with vertices at ({a}, {y}), ({b}, {y}), and ({x}, {c}), where the base is horizontal and height is |{c} - {y}|?
templates.generation_note: Base = abs(b-a). Side lengths use distance formula only for the two slant sides.

templates.problem: Segment AB goes from ({ax},{y}) to ({bx},{y}). Segment CD goes from ({cx},{y2}) to ({dx},{y2}). Which segment is longer?
Find the mean of this data set: {a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, {e}
Find the median of: {a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, {e}
Find the range and mode of: {a}, {b}, {c}, {a}, {d}, {b}
A student scored {a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, and {e} on five tests. What is their mean score? What score would they need on a 6th test to raise the mean to {f}?
Is this question statistical or not statistical: '{q}'? Explain.
question_bank: How tall are students in our class?
How many hours do students sleep per night?
What are the favorite colors of 6th graders?

templates.problem: Class A test scores: mean = {a}, median = {b}. Class B test scores: mean = {c}, median = {d}. Which class performed better overall? Explain.
The mean of four numbers is {m}. Three of the numbers are {a}, {b}, and {c}. What is the fourth number?
A student found the median of {a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, {e} by averaging the first and last values. What is wrong with this approach? What is the correct median?
Create a dot plot for this data: {a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, {e}, {a}, {b}, {c}. Describe the distribution in one sentence.
Find the mean absolute deviation (MAD) for: {a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, {e}. First find the mean, then the average distance from the mean.
Daily high temperatures for a week: {a}, {b}, {c}, {d}, {e}, {f}, {g}°F. Find the mean, median, and range. Which measure best describes a typical day?
Create a data set of {n} numbers that has a mean of {m} and includes at least one negative value.

skills.name: Preview: Proportional Relationships (7th Grade Intro)
skills.note: Introduces 7th grade concept of constant of proportionality at accessible level. Problems use simple numbers and avoid formal y=kx notation.

templates.problem: A car travels {a} miles in 1 hour, {b} miles in 2 hours, {c} miles in 3 hours. Is this a proportional relationship? How do you know?

constraints.note: Half the time make it proportional (b=2a, c=3a). Half make it not proportional.

templates.problem: For every {a} hours worked, a person earns ${b}. How much do they earn per hour? How much for {c} hours?

A faucet drips {a} gallons every {b} minutes. Complete this table and describe the pattern:
| Minutes | 1 | {b} | {c} | {d} |
| Gallons | ? | {a} | ? | ? |
{a} pounds of apples cost ${b}. At this rate, what is the cost per pound? How much would {c} pounds cost?
Plan A: ${a} per month. Plan B: ${b} for {c} months up front. Which plan costs less per month?

skills.name: Preview: Adding and Subtracting Negative Numbers in Context (7th Grade Intro)
skills.note: Students have done integer multiplication and division (6.NS). This previews the 7.NS framework of understanding p + q on a number line with direction.

templates.problem: Show {a} + ({b}) on a number line. Start at {a}, move {abs_b} units in the {direction} direction. Where do you land?
templates.generation_note: direction = left if b < 0, right if b > 0

templates.problem: It was {a}°F and the temperature changed by {b}°F. What is the new temperature? Did it get warmer or colder?
Without calculating, will {a} + {b} be positive, negative, or zero? Explain using the idea of distance on a number line.
A bank account has ${a}. The owner makes {b} transactions: {t1}, {t2}, {t3}. What is the final balance?
A number line shows a point starting at {a} and ending at {b}. What was the change? Write it as an addition equation.
